backgroundInformationList.description: Notice of Public Meeting - City of Toronto Act, 2006 Public Notice - Request to Close Parts of the public highway on portions of Dundas Street West, Kipling Avenue and Bloor Street West, in the Six Points area

noticeDescription:
CITY OF TORONTO ACT, 2006, PUBLIC NOTICE

 

Etobicoke York Community Council Area

 

The Council of the City of Toronto proposes to pass a by-law to permanently close the following:

 

Parts of the public highway on portions of Dundas Street West, Kipling Avenue and Bloor Street West, in the Six Points area, legally described as follows:

 

1. Part of the public highway known as Dundas Street West, legally described as

follows:

 

PART OF PIN 07541-0085 (LT)

 

Firstly:

PART OF LOT 6, CONCESSION 5, COLONEL SMITH’S TRACT

PART OF DUNDAS STREET, REGISTERED PLAN 6562

PART OF LOT 6, PLAN 2946

DESIGNATED AS PART 2, PLAN 66R-31342

 

Secondly:

PART OF LOT 6, CONCESSION 5, COLONEL SMITH’S TRACT

PART OF WIDENING, PART OF LANE, PART OF LOT 26,

REGISTERED PLAN 2706

ALL OF LOTS 4 AND 5 AND PART OF LOTS 1, 2, 3, 6, 7, 8, 9 AND 10, REGISTERED PLAN 3408

PART OF DUNDAS STREET, REGISTERED PLAN 6562 DESIGNATED

AS PARTS 2 AND 3, PLAN 66R-3194

 

2. Part of the public highway known as Kipling Avenue, legally described as follows:

 

PART OF PIN 07533-0042(LT)

PART OF BLOCK A, REGISTERED PLAN 3408

DESIGNATED AS PART 5, PLAN 66R-31942

 

3. Part of the public highway known as Bloor Street West, legally described as follows:

 

PART OF PIN 07541-0080 (LT)

PART OF LOT 6, CONCESSION 5, COLONEL SMITH'S TRACT GEOGRAPHIC TOWNSHIP OF ETOBICOKE

DESIGNATED AS PART 1, PLAN 66R-31942

 

City of Toronto and Province of Ontario

Land Titles Division of the Toronto Registry Office (No. 66)

 

At its meeting to be held in the Council Chamber Etobicoke Civic Centre, 399 The West Mall on July 7, 2026 at 9:30 AM, or shortly afterwards, the Etobicoke York Community Council will hear from, or by their counsel, agent or solicitor, any person who wishes to speak to this matter.

 

The meeting of the Etobicoke York Community Council will be held with members participating in person and remotely, and the proceedings of the Etobicoke York Community Council will be conducted publicly.

 

To obtain additional information, including to view a copy of the proposed by-law and plan, please review Item EY33.38 on the Community Council agenda at

https://secure.toronto.ca/council/agenda-item.do?item=2026.EY33.38.

 

To submit comments or address Etobicoke York Community Council meeting on July 7, 2026, please contact the following City official no later than 12:00 p.m. on July 6, 2026:

 

Nancy Martins

Administrator, Etobicoke York Community Council

City Clerk’s Office

Toronto City Hall

100 Queen St. W.

Toronto, ON M5H 2N2

Telephone: 416-397-4579; Fax: 416-392-2980

E-mail: etcc@toronto.ca

 

Special Assistance for Members of the Public:  City staff can arrange for special assistance with some advance notice. If you need special assistance, please call 416-397-4579, TTY 416-338-0889 or e-mail etcc@toronto.ca.

 

Notice to people writing or making presentations to the Etobicoke York Community Council: The City of Toronto Act, 2006 and the City of Toronto Municipal Code authorize the City of Toronto to collect any personal information in your communication or presentation to City Council or its committees. The City collects this information to enable it to make informed decisions on the relevant issue(s). If you are submitting letters, faxes, e-mails, presentations or other communications to the City, you should be aware that your name and the fact that you communicated with the City will become part of the public record and will appear on the City’s website. The City will also make your communication and any personal information in it – such as your postal address, telephone number or e-mail address – available to the public, unless you expressly request the City to remove it.

 

Many Committee, Board, and Advisory Body meetings are broadcast live over the internet for the public to view.  If you speak at the meeting you will appear in the video broadcast.  Video broadcasts are archived and continue to be publicly available.

 

This notice shall also constitute notice to the public of the proposed public highway closure in accordance with the requirements of the Municipal Class Environmental Assessment for Schedule A+ activities.

 

If you want to learn more about why and how the City collects your information, write to the City Clerk’s Office, Toronto City Hall, 100 Queen Street West, Toronto ON, M5H 2N2 or by calling 416-397-4579.
